Title: RHA2b-mediated MYB30 degradation facilitates MYB75-regulated, sucrose-induced anthocyanin biosynthesis in Arabidopsis seedlings

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: R2R3-MYB family; N-terminal MYB DNA-binding domain
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant physiology and stress adaptation; beneficial for humans
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: High sucrose (6% sucrose); also high light in a supplementary assay
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: MYB30 transcript was not obviously altered by high sucrose, but MYB30 protein abundance decreased under prolonged high-sucrose treatment
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of MYB30 increases, and overexpression decreases, sucrose-induced anthocyanin accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Direct repressor of the key anthocyanin regulator MYB75 and inhibitor of MBW complex assembly, thereby restricting anthocyanin biosynthesis under normal/low-sucrose conditions
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MYB75; DFR, LDOX, UF3GT indirectly through MYB75/MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses MYB75 transcription directly; represses expression of anthocyanin biosynthetic genes DFR, LDOX, UF3GT and other flavonoid-related genes under high sucrose
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression; protein–protein interaction-mediated inhibition of MBW complex assembly
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis via control of MBW complex activity
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: High sucrose promotes its degradation through RHA2b; high light also associated with MYB30-dependent anthocyanin regulation
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Participates in a feedforward/self-activation loop where MYB75 activates RHA2b, RHA2b degrades MYB30, and MYB30 repression on MYB75 is relieved
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Interferes with MYB75-containing MBW complex; competes with TT8 and EGL3 for MYB75 binding
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Loss-of-function mutants; overexpression; complementation; RNA-seq; RT-qPCR; transactivation assay; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; Y2H; Co-IP; BiFC; pull-down; double-mutant analysis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. myb30 mutants accumulated more anthocyanin, whereas MYB30 overexpression reduced anthocyanin under 6% sucrose. 2. MYB30 directly bound the MYB75 promoter and repressed MYB75 transcription. 3. MYB30 physically interacted with MYB75 and inhibited MBW complex assembly by competing with TT8/EGL3. 4. MYB30 acts upstream of MYB75 because the myb75 mutation abolished the high-anthocyanin phenotype of myb30. 5. High sucrose triggered proteasome-dependent MYB30 degradation.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression lowers anthocyanin accumulation; broader transcriptomic effects included changes in flavonoid and jasmonate-related genes, implying possible pleiotropy
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for engineering anthocyanin-rich crops by reducing MYB30 activity or stability, but potential pleiotropic effects on other pathways should be considered
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in overexpression/transient assays; native MYB30 promoter in complementation
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MYB30 did not physically interact with TT8, EGL3, or MYB90 in tested assays; transcriptome also suggested regulation of flavonoid and JA-related genes

Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: R2R3-MYB transcription factor; core MBW complex component
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant physiology and stress adaptation; beneficial for humans
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: MYB75 transcript was induced by high sucrose; expression was higher in myb30 mutant and lower in rha2b mutant under high sucrose
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Core positive regulator of seedling anthocyanin biosynthesis and key component of MBW complex; required for elevated anthocyanin accumulation in myb30 background
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: RHA2b directly; DFR and other late anthocyanin biosynthetic genes via MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates RHA2b transcription directly; activates DFR promoter activity and anthocyanin biosynthetic genes as MBW component
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional activation; protein complex formation in MBW complex
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Positive feedforward/self-activation module: MYB75 activates RHA2b, which degrades MYB30 and thereby relieves repression on MYB75
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Core component of the MYB-bHLH-WD40 (MBW) complex with TT8/EGL3 and WD40 partner(s)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; double-mutant analysis; transactivation assay; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; Y2H; Co-IP; BiFC; pull-down
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MYB75 is essential for sucrose-induced anthocyanin biosynthesis, as myb75 mutant accumulated less anthocyanin. 2. MYB30 directly represses MYB75 transcription and also binds MYB75 protein to inhibit MBW complex formation. 3. MYB75 directly activates RHA2b transcription by binding its promoter. 4. The myb75 mutation suppresses the elevated anthocyanin phenotype of myb30.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong engineering target to elevate anthocyanins, potentially combined with suppression of MYB30 or activation of RHA2b for stronger sucrose-responsive accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter in transient and ChIP assays
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts both as central activator of biosynthetic genes and as inducer of RHA2b that removes its repressor MYB30

Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Plant physiology and stress adaptation; beneficial for humans
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: RHA2b transcript increased ~1.7-fold under high sucrose; induction was largely lost in myb75 mutant; protein accumulated under high sucrose in transient assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates sucrose-induced anthocyanin accumulation by promoting MYB30 degradation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Functional mediator that relieves MYB30 repression under high sucrose, enabling MYB75-driven anthocyanin biosynthesis
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Indirectly increases MYB75 and anthocyanin biosynthetic gene expression by destabilizing MYB30
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Anthocyanin biosynthesis via post-translational control of MYB30 repressor abundance
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: High sucrose; direct transcriptional activation by MYB75
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of a positive feedforward/self-activation loop: MYB75 induces RHA2b, which degrades MYB30 and further releases MYB75 function
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: Acts through direct interaction with MYB30
Regulation_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: MYB30; MYB75 (regulatory relationship, not stated as physical interaction)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Mutant analysis; double-mutant analysis; immunoblotting; CHX/MG132 assay; in vivo ubiquitination assay; split-Luc; transactivation assay; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RHA2b expression and protein accumulation are induced by high sucrose. 2. RHA2b promotes MYB30 ubiquitination and proteasomal degradation under high sucrose. 3. Loss of RHA2b attenuates MYB30 ubiquitination/degradation and lowers anthocyanin accumulation. 4. MYB75 directly binds the RHA2b promoter and activates its expression. 5. Genetic analysis places RHA2b upstream of MYB30 in this sucrose-induced anthocyanin module.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible redundancy with other E3 ligases such as MIEL1 and COP1; not all downstream genes (e.g., LDOX) responded similarly in rha2b mutant
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful positive lever for anthocyanin enhancement through conditional removal of MYB30 repression, potentially preferable for inducible or environment-responsive engineering
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native RHA2b promoter in reporter/protein accumulation assays
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: MYB30 was still partially degraded in rha2b mutant, indicating additional E3 ligases may contribute
